note: Recorded utterances that contain a spoken command, replayed through the real no-LLM dictation pipeline by `KeyScribe --commands-check benchmark/commands`. Each case asserts declaratively: `contains` = substrings that must survive (a control char \n/\t, the clipboard value, or a literal proving a command did NOT fire); `absent` = substrings that must be gone (a consumed trigger phrase, or text 'scratch that' removed); `equals` = the whole output (whole-utterance replacement, case-exact); `noLeadingPunct` = value with no clause punctuation before it (the clipboard fold). Matching is case-insensitive except `equals`. Record <id>.wav for each case in your own voice — real voice reproduces the spurious punctuation/casing engines emit far better than TTS; `say` is a quick synthetic smoke that will NOT reproduce fold/empty-segment punctuation. The clipboard VALUE below is inserted by the pipeline, never spoken.

cases.say: first thought insert new line second thought
here is the plan, insert new line, then the details
wrap up this section insert new paragraph and start fresh
the plan is done. insert new paragraph next steps below
column one insert tab character column two
please write a new paragraph about the weather now
We went up the hill, scratch that. We went down the hill.
We went up the hill. Scratch that. We went down the hill.
I think the meeting is on Tuesday scratch that
eggs, milk, bread. scratch that. we are done
I told her to scratch that lottery ticket and see if we won
Let me scratch that itch real quick

begin verbatim keep this end verbatim. Next sentence here

double check that insert clipboard contents matches what we expect
